title: Display all prices in Indian Rupees (₹) across the frontend

requirements.text: Update the price formatting utility in frontend/src/lib/utils.ts to format all prices using the Indian Rupee symbol (₹) and Indian number formatting (e.g. ₹1,499 or ₹1,499.00). Replace any existing currency formatter (e.g. USD/$ format) with one that uses 'en-IN' locale and 'INR' currency, or equivalent manual formatting. This single change must cascade to every component that calls the price formatter.

quotes: change all price tag is in indian currency- rupees

acceptanceCriteria: The formatPrice (or equivalent) utility in utils.ts outputs prices with the ₹ symbol instead of $ or any other currency symbol.
Prices on the Product Catalog page (ProductCard.tsx) display in ₹ format.
Prices on the Product Detail page display in ₹ format.
Unit price and line totals on the Cart page display in ₹ format.
Order summary prices and the subtotal/total on the Checkout page display in ₹ format.
Product prices in the Admin Panel table/card list display in ₹ format.
No page in the app shows a $ or other non-INR currency symbol for any price.

constraints: Do not change any backend price storage — prices remain as plain numbers in the Motoko actor.
Only the display/formatting layer in the frontend must be changed.
Do not modify any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Currency conversion — prices are already stored as INR values; only the display format changes.
Adding a currency switcher or multi-currency support.
Changing the backend data model or any Motoko code.
